note: The authority-anchor gate's declared data (SPEC INV-207, ROADMAP 415). A sentence carrying a person's AUTHORITY — a decision, word, or ruling attributed to the human — must name the EXCHANGE it came from, at minimum a checkable date. The attribution patterns are held here as DATA (the shipped-language allowlist's shape), so the gate's own code names no person: a host covers its own people by adding to person_names, and the role forms (his/the owner's/he decided) are person-agnostic. The gate reds an authority-claim that carries no date anchor; a genuine dated attribution passes.
note_struck: A retracted line is not a live claim. The decision-set record marks a struck entry with a STRUCK tag or ~~strikethrough~~; the gate skips a struck sentence, since the person has already reached in and struck it (that is the read-back's own action).

waivers.note: the build-lane cap of three, a genuine dated attribution already carrying its anchor — listed only if a splitter edge ever mis-scopes it
